title: Power intelligent agents with AI-native databases
summary: AI-native databases are presented as essential infrastructure for powering agents with enterprise data. The talk advocates for multimodal querying, graph retrieval-augmented generation, and adoption of open standards like Model Context Protocol to enable high-fidelity context and rapid agent deployment.
writeup: This Google Cloud Tech talk frames AI-native databases as core infrastructure for connecting agents to enterprise data. It argues that high-fidelity context, not just model capability, determines how well agents perform and how quickly they can be deployed. The concrete techniques covered include multimodal querying, graph retrieval-augmented generation, and adoption of the open Model Context Protocol standard.
